title: Inventory Demo And Replay Modules
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the four committed src/demo/ TypeScript modules that together model the vanilla DOOM 1.9 demo and replay surface: the demoFile.ts standalone Doom 1.9 lump parser (DEMO_VERSION_19=109, DEMO_HEADER_SIZE=13, DEMO_TIC_SIZE=4, DEMO_END_MARKER=0x80, DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS=4, DEMO_TIC_RATE=35) used by the WAD asset and ticcmd test surfaces, the demoParse.ts full-spectrum demo header parser that branches between the vanilla DOOM 1.9 (version=109, header=13, command=4), longtics (DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION=111, DEMO_LONG_TICS_COMMAND_SIZE=5), and pre-v1.4 old (DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_HEADER_SIZE=7, DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_MAX_SKILL=4) header variants in the same way G_DoPlayDemo selects them, the demoPlayback.ts stateful G_DoPlayDemo+G_ReadDemoTiccmd playback wrapper with the DemoPlayback class, the netDemo/netGame/playersInGame[] mutable bitmap, the singleDemo->quit completion action, and the marker-on-next-read finish ordering, and the demoRecord.ts stateful G_BeginRecording+G_WriteDemoTiccmd recorder with DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000 (131072) maxdemo cap, DEMO_RECORD_WRITE_HEADROOM=16 vanilla precheck, the longtics int16 angle path, the vanilla angle-byte rounding plus turn-carry quantization, and the trailing 0x80 finish marker, without embedding raw demo bytes from the bundled DOOM1.WAD DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lumps or any proprietary configuration data.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using git ls-files-compatible Bun.Glob scans, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints, src/demo/, test/demo/, test/parity/bundled-demo-sync.test.ts, test/playable/demo-replay/, reference/manifests/demo-lump-summary.json, reference/manifests/vanilla-limit-summary.json, reference/manifests/source-catalog.json Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 anchors, and package.json Bun script context.

modules.role: Implements the standalone Doom 1.9 demo lump parser with DEMO_VERSION_19=109 vanilla version byte, DEMO_HEADER_SIZE=13 fixed header span, DEMO_TIC_SIZE=4 per-player tic command width, DEMO_END_MARKER=0x80 trailing marker, DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS=4 player-array cardinality, and DEMO_TIC_RATE=35 tic-rate constant, the DemoTicCommand{forwardmove,sidemove,angleturn,buttons} per-player record, the DemoFile{version,skill,episode,map,deathmatch,respawn,fast,nomonsters,consoleplayer,playersPresent,activePlayers,ticCount,durationSeconds,tics} parsed-lump record, and the parseDemoLump function that walks the canonical 13-byte header followed by activePlayers*4 bytes per tic into a frozen DemoFile with throw on truncation or missing 0x80 marker.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c G_DoPlayDemo demo lump entry point for vanilla DOOM 1.9 demos (version byte 109) and the doomdef.h MAXPLAYERS=4 / TICRATE=35 baseline, scoped to the single DOOM 1.9 versioned-demo branch without longtics or pre-v1.4 header variants.

modules.role: Implements the full-spectrum G_DoPlayDemo header branching parser with DEMO_END_MARKER=0x80 trailing marker, DEMO_VANILLA_VERSION_19=109 vanilla version byte, DEMO_VANILLA_HEADER_SIZE=13 / DEMO_VANILLA_COMMAND_SIZE=4 vanilla layout, DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION=111 non-vanilla version byte, DEMO_LONG_TICS_COMMAND_SIZE=5 longtics command width with int16 angleTurn, DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_HEADER_SIZE=7 / DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_MAX_SKILL=4 pre-v1.4 leading-byte branch (skill <= 4 is treated as a no-version old demo), DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS=4 player slots, and DEMO_TIC_RATE=35 tic-rate, the DemoFormat 'longtics'|'old'|'vanilla' discriminator, the DemoTicCommand{angleTurn,buttons,forwardMove,sideMove} normalized-int16 record, the ParsedDemo{activePlayerCount,commandByteLength,commandsByTic,consolePlayer,deathmatch,durationSeconds,endMarkerOffset,episode,fastMonsters,format,headerByteLength,map,noMonsters,playersInGame,respawnMonsters,skill,ticCount,versionByte} unified parsed-demo record, and the parseDemo first-byte format dispatcher that decodes either the vanilla shifted-byte angle (raw_byte<<8 to int16) or the longtics int16 LE angle and terminates on the trailing 0x80 marker.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c G_DoPlayDemo header dispatch (treating leading bytes 0..4 as the skill field of a pre-v1.4 no-version demo, otherwise reading versionByte then routing to the longtics int16-angle path when versionByte == DOOM_191_VERSION = 111) and the matching G_ReadDemoTiccmd per-tic decoder for the three demo header variants.

modules.role: Implements the stateful G_DoPlayDemo+G_ReadDemoTiccmd playback wrapper with the DemoPlayback class, DEMO_PLAYBACK_DEFAULT_VERSION=DEMO_VANILLA_VERSION_19=109 expected version baseline, the DemoPlaybackCompletionAction 'advance-demo'|'none'|'quit' G_CheckDemoStatus result discriminator, the DemoPlaybackOptions{allowOldFormat,allowLongTics,expectedVersionByte,netDemo,soloNet,singleDemo} -netdemo/-solo-net/singledemo/longtics gates, the DemoPlaybackSnapshot{completionAction,consolePlayer,deathmatch,demoplayback,fastMonsters,netDemo,netGame,noMonsters,playersInGame,respawnMonsters,singleDemo,ticIndex} immutable view, the netdemo derivation from playersInGame[1] || netDemo || soloNet, the readNextTic streaming reader that returns the final real tic and only finishes playback on the next read past ticCount (matching G_ReadDemoTiccmd's marker-on-next-read ordering), and the finishPlayback reset that clears consolePlayer/deathmatch/fastMonsters/netDemo/netGame/noMonsters/respawnMonsters and unsets playersInGame[1..3] before transitioning demoplayback to false.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c G_DoPlayDemo, G_ReadDemoTiccmd, and G_CheckDemoStatus demo playback state machine with the netdemo/netgame derivation from playersInGame[1] and the -netdemo/-solo-net/singledemo command-line gates, plus the marker-on-next-read final-tic ordering preserved by the vanilla reader before D_AdvanceDemo or quit completion runs.

modules.role: Implements the stateful G_BeginRecording+G_WriteDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus recorder with the DemoRecorder class, DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000 (131072) MAXDEMOSIZE buffer cap, DEMO_RECORD_WRITE_HEADROOM=16 vanilla precheck (Doom stops recording before a tic when fewer than 16 bytes remain), the DemoRecordCommand{angleTurn,buttons,chatCharacter?,consistency?,forwardMove,sideMove} input record (chatCharacter and consistency are savegame-only ticcmd_t fields intentionally ignored by the demo writer), the DemoRecorderOptions{consolePlayer,deathmatch,episode,fastMonsters,format,map,maximumSize,noMonsters,playersInGame,respawnMonsters,skill,vanillaDemoLimit,versionByte} header initialization, the format-aware writeHeader that emits 13 bytes for vanilla/longtics and 7 bytes for old (no version byte, no flag bytes), the recordCommand pipeline that runs the vanilla angle quantizer (rounded byte angle plus turn-carry feedback) for non-longtics formats and writes int16-LE angles for longtics, the vanilla-limit gating that returns null on a precheck overflow without throwing, and the finish writer that appends the trailing 0x80 marker and returns a sliced demo buffer.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c G_BeginRecording, G_WriteDemoTiccmd, and G_CheckDemoStatus demo recorder with the MAXDEMOSIZE=0x20000 / vanilla_demo_limit precheck (Demo_Headroom=16) and the angle byte-quantization + turn-carry feedback path used to round the int16 angleTurn into a single byte for non-longtics demos.

test_coverage.notes: test/demo/ covers the full-spectrum G_DoPlayDemo header dispatch (vanilla DOOM 1.9 versioned, longtics, and pre-v1.4 old) and the G_ReadDemoTiccmd per-tic decoder under demo-parse.test.ts, the G_DoPlayDemo+G_ReadDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus DemoPlayback streaming reader and marker-on-next-read finish ordering under demo-playback.test.ts, and the G_BeginRecording+G_WriteDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus DemoRecorder buffer cap, vanilla angle quantizer, and round-trip-through-parseDemo coverage under demo-record.test.ts. The parity-lane test/parity/bundled-demo-sync.test.ts exercises the parseDemo decoder against the bundled DOOM1.WAD DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lump sequences as a parity oracle harness, and four playable-contract tests under test/playable/demo-replay/ exercise the DemoPlayback class through the title loop and clean-launch replay surface; the demoFile.ts legacy single-format lump parser is exercised through test/wad/misc-assets.test.ts and test/input/ticcmd.test.ts at the wad and input lane test roots rather than under test/demo/.

reference_manifest_status.notes: Manifest alignment records the vanilla MAXDEMOSIZE=131072 (0x20000) demo recording buffer cap that src/demo/demoRecord.ts mirrors as DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE, the MAXPLAYERS=4 player-array cardinality that src/demo/demoFile.ts and src/demo/demoParse.ts mirror as DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS, the TICRATE=35 game-tic baseline that src/demo/demoFile.ts and src/demo/demoParse.ts mirror as DEMO_TIC_RATE and that drives durationSeconds = ticCount/35, the DOOM 1.9 versioned-demo magic byte 109 that src/demo/demoFile.ts mirrors as DEMO_VERSION_19 and src/demo/demoParse.ts mirrors as DEMO_VANILLA_VERSION_19, the non-vanilla longtics version byte 111 that src/demo/demoParse.ts mirrors as DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION, and the trailing 0x80 (128) end marker that all four modules mirror as DEMO_END_MARKER. The demo-lump-summary.json manifest catalogs the three bundled DOOM1.WAD DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lumps with skill=2 / episode=1 / map=5/3/7 / activePlayers=1 metadata and SHA-256 fingerprints (DEMO1 5026 tics, DEMO2 3836 tics, DEMO3 2134 tics) that the parity-lane bundled-demo-sync.test.ts compares parseDemo output against; this inventory does not embed raw DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lump bytes or any proprietary configuration data.

demo_and_replay_boundary_status.notes: src/demo/ owns the demo and replay surface partitioned into demo_lump (demoFile.ts) for the legacy standalone Doom 1.9 single-format lump parser (used by test/wad/misc-assets.test.ts and test/input/ticcmd.test.ts as a thin lump decoder), demo_parse (demoParse.ts) for the full-spectrum vanilla/longtics/old G_DoPlayDemo header dispatch with the matching G_ReadDemoTiccmd per-tic decoder, demo_playback (demoPlayback.ts) for the stateful DemoPlayback streaming reader that mirrors G_DoPlayDemo+G_ReadDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus including the netdemo derivation and the marker-on-next-read finish ordering, and demo_record (demoRecord.ts) for the stateful DemoRecorder that mirrors G_BeginRecording+G_WriteDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus with the MAXDEMOSIZE=0x20000 / vanilla_demo_limit precheck and the vanilla angle byte-quantization + turn-carry feedback path. Demo modules accept buffer inputs, DemoRecorderOptions, DemoPlaybackOptions, DemoRecordCommand records, and frozen ParsedDemo snapshots through injected parameters; each module is unit-tested without a live filesystem, WAD, or active game state, and the only WAD-bound entry point is the parity-lane bundled-demo-sync.test.ts harness that reads DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 from the read-only reference DOOM1.WAD.

implications: src/demo/demoParse.ts is the central parser surface that demoPlayback.ts (11 src/ importers, 5 test importers) and demoRecord.ts both build on; reshaping the DemoFormat 'longtics'|'old'|'vanilla' discriminator, the ParsedDemo{format,headerByteLength,commandByteLength,playersInGame,commandsByTic,...} record, the DemoTicCommand{angleTurn,buttons,forwardMove,sideMove} normalized record, or the DEMO_VANILLA_VERSION_19=109/DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION=111/DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_MAX_SKILL=4 header-dispatch constants is a cross-lane breaking change that the demo, playback, record, parity, and 11 playable demo-replay importer modules in src/playable/demo-replay/ must absorb together; downstream Phase 04 follow-ups 04-019 through 04-026 and the gate 04-030 depend on these exports staying stable across the inventory baseline.

src/demo/demoPlayback.ts owns the DemoPlayback class, the DemoPlaybackCompletionAction 'advance-demo'|'none'|'quit' G_CheckDemoStatus result discriminator, the netdemo derivation from playersInGame[1] || netDemo || soloNet, and the marker-on-next-read finish ordering that returns the final real tic before transitioning demoplayback to false. The 11 src/ importers under src/playable/demo-replay/ (acceptAttractLoopAndLongRunReplays, acceptCleanLaunchToGameplayReplay, captureReplayFramebufferStateAudioMusic, convertDemoInputStream, detectLongRunDrift, playBundledDemosThroughTitleLoop, preserveDemoTerminationBehavior, replayDemo1Deterministically, replayDemo2Deterministically, replayDemo3Deterministically, replayScriptedInputFromCleanLaunch) compose this class into the playable contract surface, so reshaping DemoPlaybackOptions, DemoPlaybackSnapshot, or readNextTic's null-on-finish contract is also a break of the playable demo-replay surface inventoried by 01-004 inventory-src-playable-contract-modules.

src/demo/demoRecord.ts DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000 (131072 bytes) is a bit-exact mirror of vanilla MAXDEMOSIZE=0x20000 from g_game.c that the vanilla_demo_limit compatibility flag enforces; the DEMO_RECORD_WRITE_HEADROOM=16 byte precheck and the recordCommand return-null-on-overflow contract preserve the vanilla 'demo recording stops before the next tic when fewer than 16 bytes remain' behavior. Reshaping these constants or removing the headroom precheck breaks the vanilla-demo-limit gate 04-023 enforce-vanilla-demo-size-limit and the downstream 04-024/04-025/04-026 ticcmd-stream comparisons.

src/demo/demoFile.ts is a narrower legacy parser scoped to vanilla DOOM 1.9 (version 109) lumps and is owned by the WAD asset and ticcmd test surfaces (2 test importers, 0 src importers). Its DemoTicCommand{forwardmove,sidemove,angleturn,buttons} record uses raw lump field names (lowercase, no normalization), distinct from demoParse.ts's normalized DemoTicCommand{angleTurn,buttons,forwardMove,sideMove}; consumers that need longtics support, old-format support, or the netdemo state machine must use demoParse.ts and demoPlayback.ts instead. Phase 05 follow-up 05-014 parse-demo-lumps must keep this dual-API distinction in mind so the WAD-side parser does not regress longtics or pre-v1.4 demos.

Demo modules accept Buffer inputs, DemoRecorderOptions / DemoPlaybackOptions, DemoRecordCommand and ticcmd records, and frozen ParsedDemo snapshots through injected parameters and never read the filesystem or open a WAD directly; the only WAD-bound entry point is the parity-lane test/parity/bundled-demo-sync.test.ts harness that reads DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 from the read-only reference DOOM1.WAD via the existing wad/asset modules. This boundary lets the launch and core lanes wire DemoPlayback into the title loop and DemoRecorder into the gameplay loop without leaking filesystem or WAD-decoding concerns into the demo modules; downstream Phase 10 follow-up 10-002 implement-page-ticker-and-advance-demo and the Phase 02 oracle-capture follow-ups 02-009/02-028/02-029/02-030 own the live evidence path.

src/demo/ shares a strict no-proprietary-bytes boundary with the rest of the inventory lane: the DEMO_VERSION_19=109, DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION=111, DEMO_END_MARKER=0x80, DEMO_HEADER_SIZE=13, DEMO_TIC_SIZE=4, DEMO_VANILLA_HEADER_SIZE=13, DEMO_VANILLA_COMMAND_SIZE=4, DEMO_LONG_TICS_COMMAND_SIZE=5, DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_HEADER_SIZE=7, DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_MAX_SKILL=4, DEMO_TIC_RATE=35, DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS=4, DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000, and DEMO_RECORD_WRITE_HEADROOM=16 constants are derived from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c source (S-011 in reference/manifests/source-catalog.json) and the DoomWiki Demo reference (S-041), but the inventory must never embed raw DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lump bytes or raw recorded demo bytes. Phase 02 oracle-capture follow-ups 02-009 (define-deterministic-input-stream-format) and 02-028 through 02-030 (capture-demo-N-playback-oracle) own the live evidence capture path that produces oracle artifacts under test/oracles/fixtures/.
